case_name: Tammy Crocker v. Thomas Babcock, IV, M.D., Longview Emergency Medicine Associates, Inc. and Good Shepherd Medical Center

summary.brief: Tammy Crocker v. Thomas Babcock, IV, M.D., Longview Emergency Medicine Associates, Inc. and Good Shepherd Medical Center. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Crocker, a forty-six-year-old woman, began having physical problems described by her husband, an emergency medical technician, as classic stroke symptoms.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Crocker was airlifted to Good Shepherd in Longview at 11:09 p.m. At 11:15 p.m., an in-flight crew member notified either the emergency department charge nurse or another emergency department nurse of an inbound possible stroke patient.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Although required by hospital protocol, the emergency department nurse who received the notification did not activate Good Shepherd’s stroke code protocol.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Babcock was one of three emergency department physicians on duty at the time of Crocker’s arrival.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Babcock ordered a CT scan without contrast of her brain. The scan results Babcock received at 12:32 a.m. were unremarkable.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At 12:45 a.m., Babcock consulted by telephone with Dr. Rajani Ruth Caesar, a neurologist, about the possibility of administering tissue plasminogen activator (tPA).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Crocker was ultimately diagnosed with an acute [cerebral vascular accident] with a [l]arge area of acute ischemia.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We affirm the trial court’s judgment.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Crocker began having physical problems described by her husband as classic stroke symptoms. She was alert but unable to speak, with facial droop and right side weakness.

focused_exam.neurological: Limited range of motion in right shoulder, right wrist, right knee, and right ankle. Facial droop improved during transport.

requestables.name: CT scan of the brain without contrast
requestables.description: CT scan to rule out hemorrhagic stroke

reveal.clinical_significance: Ruled out hemorrhagic stroke but did not rule out ischemic stroke
reveal.decision_impact: Should have prompted further investigation for ischemic stroke

requestables.name: Neurology consult
requestables.description: Consultation with neurologist regarding tPA administration

reveal.result_summary: Consulted with Dr. Rajani Ruth Caesar
reveal.clinical_significance: tPA not administered due to improving symptoms
reveal.decision_impact: Decision not to administer tPA based on symptom improvement

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with acute symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with suspected stroke
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management after CT scan?

options.clinical_reasoning: tPA is indicated for acute ischemic stroke within 3-hour window
Symptoms were improving, and rapid improvement is an exclusion criterion for tPA

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Improvement in symptoms suggested no acute ischemic stroke

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: tPA is standard for acute ischemic stroke within 3-hour window

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to administer tPA could lead to worsening of ischemic stroke
explanation.what_should_have_happened: tPA should have been considered more strongly given the initial presentation

patient_outcome.description: Patient diagnosed with acute cerebral vascular accident with large area of acute ischemia
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Continues to experience neurological deficits including right side weakness, facial droop, speech difficulties, and gait disturbance

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to properly diagnose and treat acute ischemic stroke
Causation established - failure to administer tPA contributed to long-term deficits

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: tPA could have reduced ischemic damage

tests_performed.test_name: CT scan of the brain without contrast

diagnoses.diagnosis: Acute cerebral vascular accident with large area of acute ischemia

treatments.outcome: Symptoms improved but long-term deficits remained

allegations.allegation: Failure to properly diagnose and treat acute ischemic stroke
